reviewer: agent matrix pass (real-inference + light/dark screenshots read, revision 2)

rubric.evidence: REAL browser run (own headless Chrome, WASM, no-GPU): first visit showed download-required + Download button; after download the loader reached ready and a real forward pass paraphrased 'The weather is lovely today, so I decided to take a long walk in the park.' → 'Today, it was lovely out, so I took a long stroll through the park to enjoy the view.' (dark run produced a different re-wording, confirming sampling variety). conformance: loads-clean=pass, calls-real-inference=pass. screenshots: reports/verify/paraphrase-generation-light.png, reports/verify/paraphrase-generation-darktheme.png
rubric.notes: Real ONNX seq2seq inference confirmed (Felladrin/onnx-chatgpt_paraphraser_on_T5_base q8) — genuine paraphrase, not a canned result, meaning preserved.

rubric.evidence: Output is a text2text-generation string that rewrites the input while preserving meaning — the correct paraphrase shape (NOT a summary, NOT a grammar fix). Live: 4 distinct re-wordings side-by-side, each with a lexical-overlap read-out (39%, 33%, 72% observed) and streamed token-by-token. screenshots: reports/verify/paraphrase-generation-light.png

conformance: declares-model-id=pass (Felladrin/onnx-chatgpt_paraphraser_on_T5_base linked to card + humarin base), declares-task=pass (text2text-generation), declares-backend=pass (WASM), declares-quantisation=pass (q8), inference-off-main-thread=pass (Web Worker + streaming). At-a-glance matches the model actually loaded.

shared-auto-init-loader=pass. REAL cached-reload test: after the ~235 MB first download, reloading auto-initialised to ready with NO Download button (reloadState=ready, reloadHasDownload=false). screenshots: reports/verify/paraphrase-generation-light-reload.png
rubric.notes: Never silently re-downloads; returning-user auto-init confirmed in-browser.

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region=pass (role=status aria-live), errors-on-the-page=pass. Fresh visit surfaced download-required with a labelled Download (~235 MB) button and the download size; no WebGPU required (WASM). Multi-model page loads a second MiniLM model behind its own honest status line. screenshots: reports/verify/paraphrase-generation-darktheme.png
rubric.notes: Offline/eviction handled by the shared loader (partial→Re-download).

rubric.evidence: Every control operable: presets, sentence textarea, how-many / temperature / max-tokens sliders (with live value labels), Sampling↔Diverse-beam segmented toggle (aria-pressed), Paraphrase button, and per-card Copy buttons (aria-labelled, clipboard). Verified via real run (Paraphrase produced 4 cards + copy). buttons-have-names=pass. screenshots: reports/verify/paraphrase-generation-light.png

Output produced by a real on-device decode (TextStreamer streams each token); confirmed by distinct sampled paraphrases across runs (light vs dark produced different re-wordings). No hardcoded result anywhere.

conformance: images-have-alt=pass, buttons-have-names=pass. Live regions (role=status aria-live) announce progress/results; paraphrase list is aria-live; segmented toggle uses aria-pressed; copy buttons carry aria-label; sliders labelled. Applied modern-web-guidance 'accessibility'.

responsive-check: desktop clean · mobile clean (no horizontal overflow, no console/network errors, both classes). Paraphrase cards use an auto-fit grid (minmax 260px) that reflows to one column on mobile; controls grid wraps. Applied 'css-layout'. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/paraphrase-generation/desktop.png, reports/responsive/screens/paraphrase-generation/mobile.png

Inference runs off the main thread in a Web Worker with token streaming (inference-off-main-thread=pass), so the UI stays responsive during the multi-second T5 decode; N paraphrases run sequentially without blocking the main thread. Applied 'break-up-long-tasks'.
rubric.notes: T5-base WASM decode is inherently slow; kept off-thread + streamed for good INP.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel grid without horizontal overflow mobile desktop
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Grid with repeat(auto-fit, minmax(...)) + flexible tracks and logical properties; wrap controls to avoid narrow-viewport overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — .pp-list and .controls-grid use auto-fit minmax grids (260px / 160px), min-inline-size:0, and logical sizing; cards reflow to one column on mobile.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check: desktop clean · mobile clean (no horizontal overflow)

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible loading progress status live region
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce load/progress/ready/error in a live region; label every control; keyboard operability; use aria-pressed for toggles.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live status + result list, aria-pressed segmented decode-mode toggle, aria-labelled copy buttons, labelled sliders.

guidanceConsulted.query: copy text to clipboard button feedback
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Wire buttons to real actions with clear, accessible feedback.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — per-paraphrase Copy buttons call navigator.clipboard.writeText and show a transient 'Copied ✓' confirmation; honest 'Copy failed' on rejection.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: models/paraphrase-generation/*.html copy handlers; real run showed working copy

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Break up long main-thread tasks / yield so inference doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the multi-second T5 decode runs off the main thread in a Web Worker and streams tokens; N paraphrases are awaited sequentially so the UI never blocks.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.

openQuestions: Does transformers.js gain real num_return_sequences>1 support in a later version (would let one call yield N paraphrases instead of N calls)?
Would exposing top-p/top-k sliders (currently fixed at 0.95/60) add value or just clutter for most users?

followUpGoals.goal: Add a 'back-translation drift' comparison to the wild page — paraphrase vs a translate→translate round-trip — to contrast two ways of rewording.

summary: Revision-2 agent matrix pass for paraphrase-generation (text2text-generation, Felladrin/onnx-chatgpt_paraphraser_on_T5_base, WASM q8). Real in-browser inference confirmed (genuine meaning-preserving paraphrases, sampling variety across runs); cached reload auto-inits with no Load button; console clean; light+dark screenshots read; desktop+mobile responsive clean. Correctly framed as paraphrasing (not summarization/grammar). Support flipped to ok on both classes.
